creator: borjam@sarenet.es
text:
(In reply to Marián Černý from comment #47)

I agree with you. Ports are an extremely useful mechanism and one of the key aspects that make FreeBSD much more useful than other systems. 

Many software packages have important compile time options. Which of them are used on built packages? Do they suit your configuration? Do you need those options with the added dependency complexity?

Especially for critical software such as bind it should be possible to build a port without any hassles.

I guess few of us are still building software for ports. Maybe people get too comfortable with packages even when it means losing functionality. I remember when everyone used the Apache prefork engine despite being much less effective just because packages were built for prefork.
